title: ocotea bracteosa
intro: Ocotea bracteosa, also known as Canelo or Mexican Sandalwood, is a plant species native to Central America and Mexico, belonging to the Lauraceae family. Its roots and bark have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Ocotea bracteosa include relieving anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and aiding in digestion. The herbal preparations of Ocotea bracteosa include essential oils extracted from its roots and bark, which can be used in aromatherapy, and teas or infusions made from its leaves and bark, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
